citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
There are four bottles of expired medications used for anesthetic procedures and sedation located in the controlled drug
safe. Torphadine, Contrased, Diazepam, and Haloperidol are mixed in with all the other currently used medications. The
use of expired medications is not considered to be a safe or appropriate method to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat
diseases and injuries since the expected results cannot be guaranteed. In this case, these medications can be used to
treat painful or stressful conditions in animals. The facility must ensure the use of appropriate methods to prevent, control,
diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. The licensee must ensure all medications on-hand are not expired. The facility
corrected this at time of inspection on May 11, 2026, by throwing expired medications away.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
There are four bottles of expired medications used for anesthetic procedures and sedation located in the controlled drug 
safe. Torphadine, Contrased, Diazepam, and Haloperidol are mixed in with all the other currently used medications. The 
use of expired medications is not considered to be a safe or appropriate method to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat 
diseases and injuries since the expected results cannot be guaranteed. In this case, these medications can be used to 
treat painful or stressful conditions in animals. The facility must ensure the use of appropriate methods to prevent, control, 
diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. The licensee must ensure all medications on-hand are not expired. The facility 
corrected this at time of inspection on May 11, 2026, by throwing expired medications away. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative. 
 
 n
